name: Rapido
summary: Two or three sentences explaining what this company does and why it matters. Reference one or two key facts — a metric, a market, a bold claim — to hook the reader.
metaDescription: FounderLens strategic company analysis of Rapido.
pageTitle: FounderLens · Company Name — Strategic Company Analysis

sections.title: What Actually Does
sections.lead: One or two compelling italic sentences that set the stage for this section. Write it as if explaining to a smart friend.
sections.body: The main body text for this section. Can include bold, highlighted terms, and inline Feature Tags. Add a second paragraph here if needed.

callout.label: Why It Matters
callout.body: A compact, high-impact insight in 2–3 sentences. End with a punchy takeaway.

sections.title: A Problem Hidden in Plain Sight
sections.lead: Why this problem is bigger and older than people realise.
sections.body: Describe the problem the company solves, the market gap, the pain points.

list.label: Option A
list.desc: What most people tried and why it failed.

list.label: Option C
list.desc: What happened when people gave up.

callout.label: The Hidden Opportunity
callout.body: The insight the founders saw that others missed. Market Gap

sections.title: Why Now Was Exactly Right
sections.lead: The macro forces that made this moment inevitable.
sections.body: Explain the timing: policy tailwinds, demographic shifts, technology changes, or post-pandemic behaviour.

callout.label: The Key Shift
callout.body: The one structural change that made this business model newly viable. Bold the key claim. Proactive Strategy

sections.title: How Makes Money
sections.lead: The model explained plainly — no jargon, no consultant-speak.
sections.body: Explain the revenue mechanics in prose before listing them.

list.label: Revenue Stream 1
list.desc: What it is and who pays for it.

list.label: Revenue Stream 2
list.desc: The recurring layer that drives LTV.

callout.label: Why the Model Works
callout.body: The economic insight that makes this model durable. Unit Economics

sections.title: What Makes Them Hard to Beat
sections.lead: Not every advantage is a moat. Here's what actually is.
sections.body: Describe the compound advantages — the things that reinforce each other and get stronger over time.

quote.text: An optional direct quote from the founder or a customer that crystallises the company's philosophy.

callout.label: The Compounding Moat
callout.body: Why the lead gets harder to close over time. This is what investors miss. Network Effects

verdict.label: 06 — FounderLens Verdict
body: The opening paragraph of the verdict. Explain what makes this company worth studying — not just the metrics, but the decision-making behind them.
The second paragraph explores what competitors struggle to replicate, and why the combination of factors is greater than the sum of its parts.
The third paragraph zooms out to the market — why it's permanent, growing, or structurally advantaged. Connect the company's present to its long-term potential.
Close with the final judgement. Make it memorable. One sentence that sticks. That's the verdict.
